'''Jake Burns''' (born 23 June 2000) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], 

==Background==
He was signed into the St Helens system after he impressed at the amateur side, Halton Farnworth Hornets.

==Club career==
===St Helens===
Burns was initially offered a three-month train and trial deal with St Helens. At the time Burns was working as a trainee accountant. Burns was St Helens reserves player of the year in 2022. 

In 2023, he joined [[North Wales Crusaders|North Wales]] on loan where he made 13 appearances. 

In round 16 of the [[2024 Super League season]], Burns made his club debut for St Helens in their 8-6 loss against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. Burns also spent time during the 2024 season on loan at [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nextgen-jake-burns-st-helens-super-league/|title=Meet the trainee accountant now living his Super League dream with St Helens|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/24499545.jake-burns-scores-two-tries-second-st-helens-appearance/|title=Jake Burns scores two tries in second St Helens appearance|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/teams/first-team/jake-burns/|title=Jake Burns shows the path to First Team is not linear|website=www.saintsrlfc.com}}</ref>
